description: Make the coderef-core MCP server repo-agnostic: it should resolve and serve whichever repo it is invoked against (per-request / cwd / arg-driven project root), not be hard-wired to a single indexed project. Today the MCP is bound to one repo's .coderef graph (observed: codebase_summary / hotspots / what_exports all return CODEREF-CORE's own graph regardless of the caller's project), so a consumer wanting the census of automation/, ASSISTANT/, or any other indexed tree cannot get it through the MCP. Fix so the server selects the target repo's .coderef graph dynamically (e.g. a project_root parameter on each tool, or resolution from the caller's working directory / a configurable root), keeping the query surface (codebase_summary, what_exports/imports/calls, find_element, hotspots, cycles, path_between, pack_context, rag_search, etc.) identical but per-repo.

scope_details.problem: MCP server hard-wired to CODEREF-CORE's .coderef graph; cannot query any other indexed project's census.
scope_details.observed_limitation: All 14 MCP tools (codebase_summary, what_exports, what_imports, what_calls, find_element, find_all_references, hotspots, cycles, path_between, pack_context, rag_search, source_of, impact_of, validation_status) return CODEREF-CORE graph regardless of caller's project.
scope_details.root_cause: Graph resolution hard-coded in coderef-mcp-server.ts (entry point) or the query layer — currently loads `.coderef/graph.json` from CODEREF-CORE only.
scope_details.solution_shape: Add dynamic project_root resolution to each MCP tool, with three tiers: (1) explicit project_root parameter (caller chooses), (2) cwd-inferred root (walk up from caller's cwd to find .coderef), (3) configurable fallback (env var / config file). All tools keep identical query contract; only the graph source changes.
scope_details.affected_surface: src/cli/coderef-mcp-server.ts (the MCP entry point) + src/pipeline/orchestrator.ts (the query dispatch layer) + potentially src/scanner/scanner.ts if graph loading is there.
scope_details.test_surface: Any indexed project (automation/, ASSISTANT/, primary-sources/) should be queryable via the repo-agnostic MCP; today only CODEREF-CORE works.
scope_details.blocking_use_case: The standards-identify --with-inventory bridge (doc-gaps + scatter detection) cannot feed .coderef census into other projects' gap analyses until the MCP serves any repo.
